assertions.text: Output contains at least 2 separate markdown tables (not everything crammed into one table)
assertions.evidence: Three separate markdown tables are present: 'Plan Pricing', 'Free Trial', and 'Cancellation and Refunds'

assertions.text: The tables cover distinct concerns — e.g. pricing/plans is separate from cancellation/refund rules
assertions.evidence: Table 1 covers plan pricing, Table 2 covers free trial behaviour, Table 3 covers cancellation and refund rules — each is a distinct concern

assertions.text: At least one output column in each table ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Table 1: 'Price?'; Table 2: 'Trial duration?', 'Auto-converts to?'; Table 3: 'Refund given?' — all tables have at least one output column ending with '?'

assertions.text: The 24-hour no-refund exception is included as a distinct scenario row
assertions.evidence: Row in Cancellation table: 'Cancel within 24h of renewal charge | ≤ 24 hours | No refund'

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'boolean', 'int', 'isProrated', camelCase identifiers
assertions.evidence: All column names use business language: 'Plan', 'Billing cycle', 'Price?', 'New subscriber?', 'Trial duration?', 'Auto-converts to?', 'Scenario', 'Time since renewal charge', 'Refund given?' — no implementation terms present
